title: salvia carnosa var. compacta
intro: Salvia carnosa var. compacta, also known as compact bush sage, is a compact and shrubby variety of the Salvia carnosa plant, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, primarily for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of essential oils, particularly salvinol and carnosic acid. The medicinal properties of Salvia carnosa var. compacta are believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and providing antioxidant effects. In herbal preparations, the leaves of this plant can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support respiratory health, soothe sore throats, and alleviate minor digestive issues.
